# c64

> Zero-install browser Commodore 64 assembly development, software emulation, sharing, and
> standard PRG/D64 interoperability.

Current state: architecture and repository specialization only. The application, emulator,
assembler, examples, build, tests, and deployment are not implemented yet.

## Start here

- [Mission](docs/MISSION.md): purpose and explicit software-only boundary
- [System](specs/SYSTEM.md): architecture, data flows, critical path, implementation status
- [Runtime status](status/SYSTEM-STATUS.md): current versus planned build/deployment truth
- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md): product invariants and review workflow

## Layer contracts

- [Emulator](specs/EMULATOR.md): NMOS 6510, C64 bus/banking, deterministic native/WASM core
- [VIC-II](specs/VIC-II.md): PAL/NTSC raster timing and video output
- [SID/CIA/input](specs/IO.md): audio, timers, ports, keyboard, joystick, IEC-facing signals
- [Code generation](specs/CODEGEN.md): browser/Node assembler, PRG and run-entry behavior
- [Media](specs/MEDIA.md): standards-compatible PRG/D64 generation and import
- [ROM assets](specs/ROM-ASSETS.md): replacements, user-supplied ROMs, licensing and privacy
- [Web client](specs/WEB-CLIENT.md): static IDE, URL sharing, autosave and presentation

## Hard boundaries

- No runtime backend, accounts, database, secrets, private shares, or in-app publishing.
- No copyrighted Commodore ROMs in the repository or generated site.
- No custom transfer hardware, firmware, PCB/HDL, KiCad, GAL/address-decode, or other
  physical 3RIC work.
- GitHub pull requests are the only canonical publishing path; only `ebadger` merges.
